Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Analyze the Request: Source Material: A detailed natural language description of a complex, multinational smartphone supply chain (R&D, sourcing from Taiwan/Japan/South Korea/Germany/US, assembly in Malaysia, global distribution via air/sea, JIT vs. aggregated shipments, regional DCs, last-mile delivery, quality/compliance/recalls, digital systems, ML/data analytics, multi-currency/timezone/regulatory context). Output: 20 thought-provoking, open-ended questions. Focus Areas: Discovering insights, potential issues, strategies for improvement, implications of decisions. Constraint: Do not provide any SQL queries; just list the questions.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Deconstruct the Source Material & Brainstorm Angles: Sourcing (Taiwan, Japan, SK, Germany, US): Geopolitical risks, single-sourcing vs. multi-sourcing, lead time variability, quality metric alignment across borders. Assembly (Malaysia, 24/7, human + automated): Labor laws, automation ROI, quality fatigue in continuous operations, scaling up/down. Transportation (Air vs. Sea, JIT vs. Aggregated): Cost vs. speed trade-offs, carbon footprint, customs delays, insurance costs for high-value components. Distribution & Last-Mile (DCs globally, retail vs. e-com vs. VIP): Demand signal distortion (bullwhip effect), handling returns/warranties, VIP fulfillment impact on standard operations. Oversight & Tech (Inventory tracking, ML forecasting, digital systems, currencies/timezones): Data silos, ML model degradation, currency hedging, system integration failures. Compliance & Risk (Environmental, labor, recalls, carbon): Recall logistics, green supply chain costs, regulatory divergence between markets.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 3. Drafting the Questions (Iterative Process aiming for 20 distinct, open-ended questions): Draft 1 (Sourcing/Risk): Given the reliance on microprocessors from Taiwan, how might escalating geopolitical tensions alter the companys sourcing strategy, and what are the cost and lead-time implications of near-shoring or diversifying these components? Draft 2 (Assembly/Automation): In the 24/7 Malaysian assembly plant, what is the optimal balance between automated machinery and human labor, and how does this balance affect defect rates and worker fatigue across multiple shifts? Draft 3 (Transportation/JIT vs. Bulk): When transitioning a specific market from aggregated monthly shipments to a just-in-time (JIT) replenishment strategy, what are the hidden costs and potential disruptions to the global logistics network? Draft 4 (Tech/ML): While machine learning models are used for demand forecasting, how does the supply chain team mitigate the risks of model drift or black swan events (like a sudden pandemic or natural disaster) that historical data cannot predict?

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 4. Review and Refine: Check against constraints: 20 questions? Yes. Open-ended? Yes. Thought-provoking? Yes. Focus on insights, issues, strategies, implications? Yes. No SQL? Yes. Just a list? Yes. Ensure variety: Covered sourcing, assembly, transport, forecasting, tech, legal, sustainability, after-sales, crisis management, etc. No obvious duplicates.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 5. Final Output Generation. (Proceed to generate output based on refined drafts).
